fact: In MUI + TanStack Query + React Router apps, ThemeProvider must be outermost, QueryClientProvider middle, BrowserRouter innermost. Inverting this order violates the architecture constraint and will be caught by adversarial review.
why: MUI theme must wrap everything so components can access the theme; React Query context wraps routing so query state persists across route changes.

fact: Use jest.config.js (not jest.config.ts) for NestJS projects. ts-node is not a standard NestJS devDependency, so a .ts config file will fail to execute without it.
why: Jest loads config files directly; .ts requires ts-node to transpile first, which must be explicitly installed.

fact: Playwright smoke tests require a running dev server to execute. In scaffold/CI context without a live server, verify test file validity via tsc --noEmit instead. Add webServer config to playwright.config.ts when live testing is needed.
why: Playwright cannot navigate to a URL if no server is listening — tests will time out rather than fail meaningfully.

fact: For postgres:16-alpine in docker-compose, use healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U <user> -d <db>"], interval: 5s, timeout: 5s, retries: 5. This reliably detects when PostgreSQL is ready to accept connections.
why: pg_isready is the canonical PostgreSQL readiness check; it exits 0 only when the server is ready to accept connections on the given user/db.

fact: When an architecture constraint lists providers or wrappers as 'A + B + C', treat listing order as outermost-to-innermost nesting order. Adversarial reviewers will flag any inversion as a BLOCKING issue.
why: Provider listing order in spec documents implies dependency nesting — outer providers must be available to inner ones.
